content:
Some things to check...

- Check your calendars and address books to see if they are trying to synchronize with unknown, unremembered, or suspicious providers; Also see if you have any add-ons for syncing calendars, address books, etc.

- Check the setting <Settings | Composition | Attachments> and see if it's set to offer to share files at a storage provider.

- Maybe you have an open email that's trying to access icloud? Check <Settings | Privacy & Security | Mail Content>. Is "Allow remote content in messages" enabled? That's a general security risk, although there is the "Exceptions" button for you to indicate trusted sources.

content:
If you have checked to make sure your server settings are correct, according to your email provider's recommendations, I'm wondering if something is running in the background that has inserted itself between Thunderbird and the network ports it's trying to use. This could be an anti-virus program or malware. If you use an AV, can you temporarily disable it for testing purposes? Do you use any other "helper" programs, like a separate spam filter, or a VPN? Or does your home gateway/router have any port forwarding instructions defined (something you would have done yourself, so probably not.)

You can try restarting Thunderbird in Troubleshooting mode, which temporarily disable all add-ons and may indicate whether that's the source of the problem. See Help | Troubleshoot Mode.

You don't mention your computer platform, but in Windows you can use Task Manager to get an idea of what is running when you get the error message, which might give a clue. There's also a more techie utility by Nirsoft called CurrPorts that will tell you what local programs are accessing what ports. Other operating systems probably have equivalent utilities.

That's all I have for now. Very mysterious.

content:
Since you mention Eset, have you tried temporarily disabling it to see if it's tying up a Thunderbird data file?

This doesn't quite fit your scenario but it's worth checking... Windows 10 and 11 have a feature called "Fast Startup" which does, indeed, make boot-up a little faster, but at the cost of not thoroughly cleaning up processes from the previous session. The consequence is that if something crashes and even if you reboot, the crashed program's process may still be running in the background! This could cause an "already running" error like what you see. IMO the feature creates more problems than it solves and I always turn it off. To do that:

To disable Fast Startup on Windows 11, use these steps:

- Open Control Panel. - Click on Hardware and Sound. - Click on Power Options. - Click the "Choose what the power button does" option. - Click the "Change settings that are currently unavailable" option. - Under the "Shutdown settings" section, UNcheck the "Turn on fast startup" option. - Click the Save changes button.

content:
Thunderbird works just fine with both of those providers, but and this is a big but. Third party software like antivirus products routinely block account creation.

My guess is you are either using a third party antivirus instead of Windows defender, or one of the popular VPN products which appear to have all sorts of issues with email, and email providers like Google have all sorts of issues with them.

Let's start simple. Is Thunderbird telling you the settings for outlook.com have been found? or is it probing for them? Frequently firewalls present a problem so Thunderbird simply can not look up it's database on the internet.

The settings for icloud are here https://autoconfig.thunderbird.net/v1.1/icloud.com and outlook are here. https://autoconfig.thunderbird.net/v1.1/outlook.com

So the issues are not that it does not work, even if it is not working for you.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
You mentioned time machine and I read warranty void. Just so you know, time machine is very good at corrupting Thunderbird profiles.

I am guessing, because I really get nothing from being told about how old your computer is, or what operating system you currently use. I am sure there are times when that may be relevant. This is not one of them. Then you are using out of date unsupported version of Thunderbird. Thunderbird supported ESR version is 140 and has been for almost a year. (July 25 V140 became the ESR release.)

But I will guess.

Thunderbird V102 came with a new address book https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/new-thunderbird-102#w_manage-your-contacts-with-ease-new-address-book

More recent versions come with this button above the list of address books This icon on the left offers a menu of address book type actions The one on the right opens a mailing list management dialog. In this place you can create a new mailing list and add contacts to it. Right clicking and existing mailing list offers an edit option as shown above.

Now to be clear, address lists contain a reference to a contact in the parent address book. They are not discreete entries, if you drag someone from another address book, they will ba added to the perent address book. Generally they are set to add an additional contact, not move the original.

What you manage to do with contacts outside of the mailing list process is a contact thing and has nothing to do with the list. I find no issue dragging and dropping a contact from the address book to the relevant mailing list.

content:
V Mozilla Thunderbird si můžeš konkrétní e-mailovou adresu (nebo celou doménu) přidat mezi „důvěryhodné“, aby už nekončila ve spamu. Tady jsou nejjednodušší způsoby:

1. Přidání do adresáře (nejúčinnější) Thunderbird považuje kontakty z adresáře za bezpečné.

Postup: Otevři ten e-mail, který padá do spamu Klikni pravým tlačítkem na odesílatele (e-mailovou adresu) Zvol „Přidat do adresáře“ (nebo „Upravit kontakt“) Ulož

Pokud máš zapnuté „důvěřovat adresáři“, už nebude chodit do spamu.

2. Zapnout důvěru adresáři

Zkontroluj nastavení: Nástroje → Nastavení účtu Vyber svůj e-mailový účet Sekce Nevyžádaná pošta Zaškrtni: „Nedůvěřovat nevyžádané poště od kontaktů v adresáři“

3. Vytvořit filtr (spolehlivé řešení)

Pokud to Thunderbird stále ignoruje: Nástroje → Filtry zpráv Vyber účet → Nový Nastav: Podmínka: „Odesílatel obsahuje … (email)“ Akce: „Přesunout zprávu do → Doručená pošta“ Ulož
